data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7fa94949">
    <title>fix(try): declined_needs_decomposition uses execution-eligible=false not time-park</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269 sets a 24h cooldown on
  declined_needs_decomposition outcomes. This violates TD-031 §5
  invariant: "execute-loop-retry-after may be set only when
  retrying the same bead after time passes could plausibly succeed
  without human/spec/dependency changes."

  Decomposition requires operator action: filing sub-beads, wiring
  dependency edges, possibly amending the parent's AC. Time elapsed
  alone changes nothing. After 24h, the drain re-attempts, fails
  again with the same "needs decomposition", and re-parks for
  another 24h. Infinite cycle until operator acts.

  Per TD-031 §5 row "parent/epic/decomposed container": the correct
  signal is execution-eligible=false (which excludes the bead from
  ordinary drain claim) plus optional decomposed label, NOT a
  time-based cooldown.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269: returns OutcomePark
    with maxAttemptCooldown (24h) for declined_needs_decomposition.
  - The bead exits ready queue for 24h based on wall-clock, then
    re-enters and fails again with the same outcome.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269: change the
    declined_needs_decomposition branch:
      - Set Extra["execution-eligible"] = false (matches TD-031 §5
        parent/epic container row).
      - Optionally add label "needs-decomposition" or
        "triage:decomposition-required" for operator visibility.
      - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released)
        with execution-eligible=false flag.
      - DO NOT set execute-loop-retry-after.
  - The bead drops out of ordinary drain claim (execution-eligible
    filter excludes it) but is visible in `ddx work focus` for
    operator triage.
  - When the operator decomposes the bead (files children, wires
    deps), they unset execution-eligible=false; bead re-enters the
    queue.

  Tests:
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NoCooldown: assert no
       execute-loop-retry-after is set.
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotEligible: assert
       execution-eligible=false is set.
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotInReady: assert the bead
       is excluded from Store.Ready() output.

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementing the operator decomposition workflow (separate
    concern; assume operators already do this).
  - Auto-decomposition (separate beads, e.g., ddx-008d288f).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(TD-031): codify push_failed/push_conflict
    outcome rows" — the same spec invariant motivates this fix.
  - Sister bead R1 (push_failed no cooldown) — same pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go declined_needs_decomposition branch (around line 256-269) sets execution-eligible=false instead of cooldown; no execute-loop-retry-after is written.
2. TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NoCooldown asserts no execute-loop-retry-after.
3. TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotEligible asserts execution-eligible=false is set.
4. TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotInReady asserts the bead is excluded from Store.Ready() output (because of the eligibility flag, not a wall-clock cooldown).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, spec:TD-031, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="7382fb2817f92c3bc803f2cd05bf694735ab0a7e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T165103-46344a65"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All four tests pass. The commit is in place.

**Summary of changes:**

- **`cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go`**: Changed the `declined_needs_decomposition` branch from setting a 24h cooldown to calling `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus` with `execution-eligible=false`, with no `RetryAfter` set.

- **`cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go`**: Updated the existing test to assert no cooldown is set; added `mutatedBead` capture to the mock store; added three new tests (`TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NoCooldown`, `TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotEligible`, `TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotInReady`) plus a `beadStoreAdapter` helper for the real-store test.

- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`**: Updated `TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` to assert `execution-eligible=false` and no cooldown, and confirmed the bead stays out of the queue via the eligibility flag.
